title: Mirabilis hirsuta
intro: Mirabilis hirsuta, also known as Hairy Four O'Clock, is a perennial plant native to the Americas, characterized by its hairy stems and bright orange flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Mirabilis hirsuta include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Mirabilis hirsuta is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional folk remedies to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Mirabilis hirsuta is a species of flowering plant in the four o'clock family (Nyctaginaceae). It is native to the Americas and is also known as hairy four o'clock or hairy beauty flower. The plant belongs to the Mirabilis genus, which includes several other species of four o'clock flowers. These plants are annuals or perennials and are characterized by their hairy stems and leaves. They produce small white or pink flowers with five petals.
uses: Mirabilis hirsuta, also known as hairy four o'clock, is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its medicinal uses include treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant is also said to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms associated with these conditions.
